name: Arashi
description: Name may derive from Papiamento 'warashi' (bonefish) — historically important in the indigenous Caquetio diet. Part of the island's 'Gold Coast' nickname for spectacular sunsets facing west over the Caribbean.

rockiness.beach: White sand with mixed limestone rock patches along the shore 🏖️
rockiness.ocean_floor: Rocky patches and small reefs within 20–40 m — best fish life near reef 🪸

live_rating.water_quality_note: Provisional estimate — not lab-tested; live water quality later

characteristics.depth_profile: Shallow sandy entry for roughly 50–100 cm over white sand; reef and rocky patches begin within 20–40 m of shore on the left (south) side when facing the sea. Depth increases gradually to 2–4 m over reef, then drops more quickly beyond the fringing reef edge.
characteristics.stone_rocks: Moderate and sporadic along the shoreline — mixed sand and low limestone rock. Rocky patches near the reef hold the best fish life. There are open sandy lanes with fewer rocks, ideal for easy entry.
characteristics.crowds: moderate; quieter on weekdays and north of the main parking area
characteristics.access: Free parking lot; Bus L10 from Oranjestad; ~15–20 min drive north from Palm Beach past California Lighthouse
characteristics.facilities: Free palapas (arrive early), snack hut, occasional chair rentals
fun_facts: Name may derive from Papiamento 'warashi' (bonefish) — historically important in the indigenous Caquetio diet.
Part of the island's 'Gold Coast' nickname for spectacular sunsets facing west over the Caribbean.

We saw a family feeding fish in the middle of a small patch, attracting a huge concentration of different species. Suddenly pelicans would sweep in and grab one or two as dinner. Having snorkel goggles underwater where a pelican might land is definitely something not to miss — but you have to time it.
Blue parrotfish, French angelfish, and green sea turtles are regular sightings on the reef to the left of the main beach.
safety_notes: Best snorkeling on calm mornings; currents can strengthen on windy days near the reef edge.
